title: The Gang Behind the Government, or Capital's Case for Industrial Unionism
description: The Gang Behind the Government, or Capital's Case for Industrial Unionism is a essay by J.W. Newbald. Barred by Customs from entering New Zealand in 1920.
descriptionParagraphs: The Gang Behind the Government, or Capital's Case for Industrial Unionism is a essay by J.W. Newbald. Barred by Customs from entering New Zealand in 1920.

What makes it interesting is the way a book becomes legible to officials as a political instrument rather than a neutral cultural object. As a essay, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in New Zealand. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Barred by Customs from entering New Zealand in 1920. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: The Gang Behind the Government, or Capital's Case for Industrial Unionism is a essay by J.W. Newbald. Barred by Customs from entering New Zealand in 1920.
banningParagraphs: The Gang Behind the Government, or Capital's Case for Industrial Unionism entered censorship debates as a essay associated with politics, public argument, and state power. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political control and political dissent.

The earliest event currently captured here is 1920 in New Zealand, where New Zealand censorship authorities classified, prohibited, or restricted. Barred by Customs from entering New Zealand in 1920. Barred by Customs from entering New Zealand in 1920.
This entry is still incomplete: more jurisdictions, court orders, and translated justifications should be added over time.

counterReadings.title: The Origins of Totalitarianism
counterReadings.author: Hannah Arendt
counterReadings.note: A foundational analysis of state terror, propaganda, and ideological conformity.

counterReadings.title: On Tyranny
counterReadings.author: Timothy Snyder
counterReadings.note: A short modern guide to resisting authoritarian politics and controlled public discourse.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
